defenses: Meter was malfunctioning or out of service at time of citation
Paid receipt or mobile app confirmation showing valid payment
Sign indicated free parking or no meter enforcement at that hour
Vehicle was not parked beyond the time shown on the meter receipt
City sticker was properly displayed but may have been obscured or fallen
Vehicle was registered outside Chicago and not subject to city sticker requirement
Sticker was purchased within the grace period allowed after registration renewal
Newly purchased vehicle within the 30-day exemption window
Street cleaning sign was missing, damaged, or obscured at the posted location
No street cleaning actually occurred on the date cited
Vehicle was moved before the street cleaning began
Incorrect block or street listed on the citation
Vehicle displays a valid residential parking permit for that zone
Permit was displayed but may not have been visible from the officer's vantage point
Zone signage was absent, damaged, or unclear
Visitor pass or temporary permit was validly displayed
Tow zone sign was missing or not visible from the parked location
Vehicle was parked in an area not clearly marked as a tow zone
Emergency circumstances required temporary stopping
Sign was recently posted without adequate notice to the public
Vehicle was parked more than the required 15 feet from the hydrant
Hydrant was not clearly visible or was unmarked
Measurement on citation is incorrect — distance exceeds the prohibited zone
Vehicle was a licensed emergency or utility vehicle with authorization
Vehicle was making a lawful temporary stop for loading or unloading
Hazard lights were on and vehicle was attended during a brief stop
No available legal parking within the block at the time of the stop
Citation location does not match where the vehicle was actually parked
Vehicle was not blocking the crosswalk — rear of vehicle did not extend into crosswalk
No crosswalk markings were present at the cited location
Citation address or location description does not match the scene
Vehicle was a licensed delivery vehicle making a required stop
Registration was current — renewal sticker was on plate but may have been obstructed
Renewal was submitted and pending but plates had not yet been updated in the system
Vehicle was recently purchased and within the temporary permit window
Plates were current but a database error caused an incorrect expired status
Citation was issued outside the posted rush hour restriction hours
Rush hour restriction sign was missing or not legible at the cited location
Vehicle was in a lane that does not have a posted rush hour restriction
Vehicle was actively loading or unloading as permitted under the restriction
